entities.summary: Single KV cache block with hash-based prefix caching identity

detail.name: Block
detail.description: Represents one fixed-size slot in the paged KV cache pool. Tracks reference count, content hash for prefix matching, and token IDs stored.
detail.why: GPU memory for KV cache must be managed in fixed-size pages to avoid fragmentation

takeaway.explain: Python: replaces __dict__ with fixed tuple of attribute names. Saves ~100 bytes per instance. Use in data-heavy classes.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: GPU memory for KV cache must be managed in fixed-size pages to avoid fragmentation
takeaway.cross_lang: C struct (fixed fields), Java record, Rust (all structs fixed by default), Go struct
takeaway.gotcha: Cannot add attributes dynamically; breaks pickle by default — implement __getstate__/__setstate__

takeaway.explain: Track how many owners share a resource. When count drops to 0, resource is freed. Used in Python GC and GPU memory.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: GPU memory for KV cache must be managed in fixed-size pages to avoid fragmentation
takeaway.cross_lang: Rust Rc<T>/Arc<T>, Swift ARC, Objective-C retain/release, C++ shared_ptr, Python default GC
takeaway.gotcha: Circular references leak memory — need weak references (weakref in Python, Weak<T> in Rust) to break cycles

takeaway.explain: Store by content hash, not location. Identical items map to same key. Enables deduplication.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: GPU memory for KV cache must be managed in fixed-size pages to avoid fragmentation
takeaway.cross_lang: Git object store, Docker layers, IPFS, Rust content-addressed crates, Nix store paths
takeaway.gotcha: Hash collisions (however rare) can cause silent data corruption — use cryptographic hashes for critical data

detail.design: Slots dataclass for minimal memory — thousands of blocks in pool
detail.smell: Token_ids is mutable but hash computed once — stale if tokens appended after hashing

entities.summary: Initialize block with empty state and zero refcount

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Creates block with no tokens, hash=0, and refcount=0. Ready for allocation.

takeaway.explain: Initialize to neutral values (0, empty). Object is valid but inactive until explicitly filled. Simplifies pool reuse.
takeaway.rationale: Creates block with no tokens, hash=0, and refcount=0
takeaway.cross_lang: Rust Default trait, Go zero values, Java default constructors, C++ value initialization
takeaway.gotcha: Zero state must be distinguishable from 'not yet initialized' — consider Option/Optional pattern

entities.summary: Record token IDs stored in this block and compute content hash

detail.name: update
detail.description: Sets block token content and computes hash for prefix caching lookup.

takeaway.explain: Hash of block contents enables O(1) lookup for duplicate blocks. Same tokens → same hash → reuse existing cached KV.
takeaway.rationale: Sets block token content and computes hash for prefix caching lookup
takeaway.cross_lang: Git content-addressed objects, Docker layer caching, Nix derivation hashing, Rust cargo fingerprinting
takeaway.gotcha: Hash computation cost must be amortized — only worthwhile if duplicates are common enough

entities.summary: Return block to empty state for reuse

detail.name: reset
detail.description: Clears tokens, hash, and refcount. Block returns to free pool.

takeaway.explain: Reset object to initial state instead of deallocating/reallocating. Avoids GC pressure and allocation cost.
takeaway.cross_lang: Java object reuse patterns, Rust mem::replace, Go sync.Pool, C++ placement new
takeaway.gotcha: Forgetting to reset ALL fields leaves stale state from previous use — prefer explicit reset() methods

entities.summary: Paged KV cache pool allocator with prefix caching

detail.name: BlockManager
detail.description: Centralizes KV cache memory allocation. Sequences request blocks, manager allocates from pool with hash-based prefix reuse.
detail.why: Multiple sequences compete for limited GPU memory — need centralized allocation with deduplication
detail.pattern: Pool allocator with prefix-cache (hash-based block reuse)

takeaway.explain: Pre-allocate N objects at startup, hand out on demand, reclaim when done. Avoids allocation overhead in hot paths.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: Multiple sequences compete for limited GPU memory — need centralized allocation with deduplication
takeaway.cross_lang: Java ThreadPoolExecutor / Apache Commons Pool, Go sync.Pool, Rust arena allocators, C++ boost::object_pool
takeaway.gotcha: Pool exhaustion under load requires strategy: block, grow, or reject. Stale objects must be health-checked

takeaway.explain: Store by content hash. Two blocks with identical tokens get same hash, enabling deduplication.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: Multiple sequences compete for limited GPU memory — need centralized allocation with deduplication
takeaway.cross_lang: Git object store, Docker layers, IPFS, Rust content-addressed crates, Nix store paths
takeaway.gotcha: Hash collisions (however rare) can cause silent data corruption — use cryptographic hashes for critical data

takeaway.explain: Divide memory into fixed-size blocks. Allocate non-contiguously with page table for indirection. Eliminates fragmentation.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: Multiple sequences compete for limited GPU memory — need centralized allocation with deduplication
takeaway.cross_lang: OS virtual memory (page tables), Java DirectByteBuffer pages, Rust mmap, Database B-tree pages
takeaway.gotcha: Internal fragmentation wastes the unused portion of the last page — tune page size to workload

detail.design: Hash table maps content hash to existing block for O(1) prefix lookup; separate free_blocks avoids scanning
detail.convention: All allocation through can_allocate/allocate pairs — never allocate without checking
detail.perf: Hot path every scheduling step; free_blocks deque gives O(1) pop

entities.summary: Create pool of N blocks and initialize free list

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Pre-allocates all blocks upfront. Free list tracks available blocks.

takeaway.explain: Allocate all resources at startup. Avoids runtime allocation latency and fragmentation. Fixed memory footprint.
takeaway.cross_lang: C malloc + placement, Java ArrayList(initialCapacity), Rust Vec::with_capacity, Go make([]T, 0, n)
takeaway.gotcha: Over-allocation wastes memory; under-allocation causes reallocation. Profile actual usage to size correctly

entities.summary: Compute content hash for a sequence of token IDs

detail.name: compute_hash
detail.description: Deterministic hash of token sequence for prefix cache lookup.

takeaway.explain: Same input always produces same hash. Required for content-addressable storage. Python hash() is NOT deterministic across runs — use hashlib.
takeaway.rationale: Deterministic hash of token sequence for prefix cache lookup
takeaway.cross_lang: Java Objects.hash (order-dependent), Rust Hash trait, Go maphash, C++ std::hash
takeaway.gotcha: Hash randomization (Python 3.3+) means hash() values differ between runs — use hashlib for persistence

entities.summary: Pop a free block from pool and increment its refcount

detail.name: _allocate_block
detail.description: O(1) allocation from free list. Block transitions from free→allocated.

takeaway.explain: Maintain list of available items. Allocate = pop from list (O(1)). Deallocate = push back. No fragmentation.

entities.summary: Decrement refcount, return block to free pool if zero

detail.name: _deallocate_block
detail.description: Reference-counted deallocation. Block only freed when no sequence references it.

takeaway.explain: Track number of owners. Decrement on release. Only free when count reaches zero. Enables safe sharing.
takeaway.cross_lang: Rust Rc<T>/Arc<T>, Swift ARC, Objective-C retain/release, C++ shared_ptr, Python default GC
takeaway.gotcha: Circular references leak memory — need weak references (weakref in Python, Weak<T> in Rust) to break cycles

entities.summary: Check if enough free blocks exist for a sequence

detail.name: can_allocate
detail.description: Predicate: sufficient free blocks for sequence needs. Called before schedule decision.

takeaway.explain: Check feasibility before attempting operation. Avoids partial failures and complex rollback. can_allocate → allocate.
takeaway.rationale: Predicate: sufficient free blocks for sequence needs

entities.summary: Assign blocks to sequence, reusing cached blocks when possible

detail.name: allocate
detail.description: Attempts prefix cache hit for each block. Falls back to fresh allocation if no cached match.

takeaway.explain: If sequence prefix matches previously computed KV, reuse those blocks. Avoids redundant computation for shared prefixes (system prompts).
takeaway.rationale: Attempts prefix cache hit for each block
takeaway.cross_lang: Trie-based caching (any language), HTTP conditional GET (ETag), memoization with partial key
takeaway.gotcha: Cache invalidation when model weights change — must version-key the cache

entities.summary: Release all blocks held by a finished sequence

detail.name: deallocate
detail.description: Decrements refcount for each block. Blocks return to free pool when refcount hits zero.

takeaway.explain: Release all resources of a completed unit at once. Simpler than incremental release, fewer error states.

entities.summary: Check if sequence can grow by one more token

detail.name: can_append
detail.description: Returns true if current block has space or a free block is available.

takeaway.explain: Before growing, verify space exists. Prevents buffer overflow in fixed-size block systems.
takeaway.rationale: Returns true if current block has space or a free block is available

entities.summary: Allocate new block if current block is full

detail.name: may_append
detail.description: Lazy allocation: only allocates new block when current one fills up.

takeaway.explain: Defer allocation until actually needed. Reduces waste when actual size < max size. Common in dynamic data structures.
takeaway.rationale: Lazy allocation: only allocates new block when current one fills up

entities.summary: Compute and store content hashes for all blocks of a sequence

detail.name: hash_blocks
detail.description: Updates hash table for prefix caching after sequence content changes.

takeaway.explain: Keep lookup structures (hash tables) updated as content changes. Enables O(1) prefix lookup at cost of O(blocks) update.
takeaway.rationale: Updates hash table for prefix caching after sequence content changes
